author: yuxu42
title: Smart CI fails: fastcore 2.0.1 breaks ghapi 1.0.4 - L.starmap AttributeError

failed_jobs.name: Smart_CI
failed_jobs.symptom: AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap' when constructing GhApi()

failed_jobs.name: ci/gha_overall_status_linux_riscv
failed_jobs.symptom: Downstream status check exits 1 due to Smart_CI failure

key_errors:
File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/ghapi/core.py", line 105, in __init__
    funcs_ = L(funcs).starmap(_GhVerb, client=self, kwargs=kwargs)
             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/fastcore/basics.py", line 578, in __getattr__
    if attr is not None: return getattr(attr,k)
                               ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap'
root_cause: The smart-ci requirements.txt pins ghapi==1.0.4 but specifies fastcore>=1.5.4 with no upper bound. fastcore 2.0.1 (released recently) was resolved and installed, but it removed the 'starmap' method from the L class (or changed it to return a plain list rather than an L instance). ghapi 1.0.4 calls L(funcs).starmap() in GhApi.__init__ (core.py line 105), which now raises AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap'. The fix is to pin fastcore to a compatible version, e.g. fastcore>=1.5.4,<2.0.0 in .github/actions/smart-ci/requirements.txt.
reproduction_steps: pip install ghapi==1.0.4 fastcore==2.0.1; python3 -c "from ghapi.all import GhApi; GhApi()"  # This reproduces the AttributeError
recommended_actions: Pin fastcore in .github/actions/smart-ci/requirements.txt to fastcore>=1.5.4,<2.0.0 to prevent future breaks from fastcore major version bumps.
Alternatively, upgrade ghapi to a version that supports fastcore 2.x.
Check if ghapi has a version pinning that explicitly declares compatibility with fastcore 2.x.
prevention_strategies: Always specify upper bounds for transitive dependencies in requirements.txt files used by CI infrastructure scripts. Consider using pip-compile or a lockfile to prevent unexpected dependency upgrades. Add a CI sanity check that verifies the smart-ci script can be imported without errors.
historical_context: No prior occurrences of this specific pattern found in the database. This is the first recorded fastcore/ghapi incompatibility failure.
